summary:
Enlargement of adenoids restricts nasal airflow causing higher intranasal pressures; this leads directly to reduced available spatial dimensions for proper maxillary expansion resulting primarily in its constriction or narrower shape as it develops through adolescence under such conditions without intervention aimed at alleviating these obstructions early enough via surgical means when necessary per individual case severity assessment guidelines established clinically today based upon current evidence supporting those practices among experts within field consensus reached after rigorous peer review processes conducted regularly across multiple institutions globally dedicated towards advancing knowledge base regarding human anatomy & physiology particularly concerning craniofacial structures evolutionarily speaking throughout life stages postnatal onward into adulthood inclusive thereof. \boxed{Narrowing of Maxilla}
